title: Weak Password Hash Algorithm
description: Using MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256 for password storage makes credentials recoverable through brute force or rainbow tables (OWASP A07:2021 / CWE-916). Password hashing requires slow, adaptive algorithms.

finding.message: Weak password hash '{{token}}' used in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use bcrypt (cost ≥ 12), Argon2id (m=65536, t=3, p=4), or scrypt for password hashing.
The Python 'secrets' module and 'passlib' library provide safe, audited implementations.
OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet provides implementation-level guidance.
NIST SP 800-63B Section 5.1.1 covers memorized secret verifiers.
